prompt:
Ultra-realistic portrait of a beautiful Korean woman leaning far back in a wooden classroom chair, both arms stretched wide open above her head in a full, uninhibited stretch — head tilted back, eyes closed, expression pure physical release and quiet euphoria. The posture of someone who has just finished something difficult. Camera positioned at eye level directly in front, capturing the full arc of the stretch.
Wearing a fitted white athletic crop top with subtle ribbing, high-waisted dark charcoal cargo-style wide-leg joggers and oversized white athletic jacket draped open — sleeves pushed to the elbows. White low-top sneakers. Silver hoop earrings. Long dark brown hair loose, cascading back and away from her face with the stretch. A graded exam paper face-down on the desk in front of her — score just barely visible.
Golden late afternoon light floods over her from the window — with arms stretched wide, her silhouette opens dramatically against the amber-lit classroom, casting a bold long shadow behind her chair. The white jacket catches bright highlights, the cargo pants deep in warm shadow. Dust motes swirling gently in the disturbed air around her.
35mm analog film grain, Kodak Portra 400 — crisp white athletic wear glowing in Portra's warmth, charcoal joggers beautifully shadowed. Hyper-realistic athletic fabric and hair motion texture. Shallow depth of field. Mood: unbounded relief, young freedom, cinematic moment of release — joyful without performance.
